blocks: The pipeline has five stages. Each one is the output of a chapter.
Chapter 6 — Working with Claude Code — Chapter 3 read the dataset. You now know the attribute types, the number of observations, the analyst's question versus

S03.meta: // SLIDE 03 — WHAT CLAUDE CODE DOES AND DOES

data.title: WHAT CLAUDE CODE DOES AND DOES NOT DO WELL
blocks: D3 v7 syntax — d3.scaleLinear(), d3.select(), joins — Chart type — defaults to whatever is most familiar (pie, line)
Layout computation — Sankey, treemap, force-directed — Channel-to-attribute mappings — which variable goes on which chan
Responsive resize — <svg viewBox> with width 100% — Sort order — defaults to source-file order
Color scale implementation — sequential, diverging, categorical — Zero baseline — only added when explicitly requested
data.section: WHAT CLAUDE CODE DOES AND DOES

data.title: THE MBTA LESSON.
blocks: Iterate on the artifact, not the specification.
The rendered chart is the truth.

S06.meta: // SLIDE 06 — COMMON FAILURES AND THE FOLLOW

data.title: COMMON FAILURES AND THE FOLLOW-UPS THAT FIX THEM
blocks: Auto-fit axis — d3.extent on quantitative scale
Wrong channel for data type — Hue ramp for quantitative variable
Wrong sort order — Source-file order on a categorical axis
Unnecessary label rotation — −30° rotation on horizontal-bar y-axis labels
